description: Top-level rights/ tree (D4): the join ledger. This is the ONLY link between a clinical KB identifier (an evidence.json source id, a rule id, a candidate id, a reference-ranges.json entry) and a rights/ record id (rights-records.json / rights-failures.json). No clinical JSON file (rules.json, candidates.json, evidence.json, reference-ranges.json) may carry an inline rights key of any kind, including under an `extensions.rights` path — that inline shape is explicitly rejected by D4 and is asserted against in tests/rights-substrate.test.mjs. EPR0-T1 creates this ledger empty; EPR0-T4 populates it while seeding the 6 triage-only rights records, joining each KB-cited source id to its rights_record_id. Entry shape: `clinical_identifier_type` names the kind of clinical identifier being joined (`evidence_source_id` per EPR0-T4; `kb_json_file_path` per EPR1-T2, below), `clinical_identifier` is the identifier value as it appears in the clinical KB JSON (a source id, or — for `kb_json_file_path` — the `modules/<id>/<filename>` artifact path itself), and `rights_record_id` is the joined entry's id in rights/rights-records.json. EPR1-T1 (FR-WP1-01) adds a second entry against the existing `evidence_source_id`/`AAP2026_IDA` identifier, joining it to a new component-scoped record for modules/anemia/reference-ranges.json (the derived-fact channel) — a deliberate second join against the same identifier, not a duplicate; scripts/validate-rights.mjs's coverage gate treats every ledger entry's rights_record_id independently, so both the source-level and component-level records stay reachable. EPR1-T2 (FR-WP1-02/03) adds the anticipated `kb_json_file_path`-shaped entries below: one per `scripts/sign-kb.mjs` `KB_JSON_FILES` artifact path (`rules.json`, `candidates.json`, `evidence.json`, `reference-ranges.json`), resolved bidirectionally by scripts/validate-rights.mjs gate (e) `checkKbJsonFileCoverage` via scripts/validate-kb.mjs's exported `resolveRightsRecordsForIdentifier`. `reference-ranges.json` joins 1:1 to its dedicated component-scoped record (RR-AAP2026_IDA-REFERENCE-RANGES, EPR1-T1); `rules.json`/`candidates.json`/`evidence.json` each join to all 6 EPR0-T4 source-level records — the fixed, already-covered set every rule/candidate evidence citation and every evidence.json source is a validated subset or member of (scripts/validate-kb.mjs's own `unknown evidence` / `sources[].id` checks) — as a structural coverage envelope, not a per-citation precision claim about which of the 6 sources each rule or candidate happens to cite today.

rights_decisions_description: EPR4-T4 (FR-WP4-04, D4): the RIGHTS-DECISION LEDGER. A separate array from `entries` above — `entries` joins a CLINICAL KB identifier to a rights_record (the coverage join, EPR0-T1+); `rights_decisions` is the shape a future rights owner (OQ-2) fills in to record their actual per-passage rights DETERMINATION, joined BIDIRECTIONALLY to (1) a rights_record_id here in rights/rights-records.json and (2) a passage id ('evidence item') in a module's evidence.json. Entry shape, deliberately identical to scripts/evidence/lib/attested-passage-map.mjs's existing attestation-entry shape so a future entry validates through that SAME reused machinery (validateAttestationEntries — the RG-9 seam) rather than a second implementation: { rights_decision_id, rights_record_id, passageId, attestedBy, credential, attestedOn, attestationRef }. `passageId` -- not `evidence_item_id` -- deliberately: a derived_synthesis item carries no rights_record of its own (RF handoff Section 9.5, decisions-block D3/D6; see scripts/rights/build-decision-brief.mjs), so a rights decision is always about a passage, the thing that actually joins to a rights_record. Validated by scripts/rights/lib/rights-decision-ledger-gate.mjs's checkRightsDecisionLedgerCoverage gate (scripts/validate-rights.mjs GATES id 'rights-decision-ledger-coverage'): the passageId/attestation-shaped half reuses validateAttestationEntries unmodified; the rights_record_id half is a plain existence-join lookup. No code path in this repository may write a live entry here (D6) -- the array ships and stays EMPTY until a named rights owner exists (OQ-2) and a real determination is recorded through whatever write path a future rights-clearance workflow specifies (see docs/project_plans/design-specs/rights-clearance-workflow.md, idea-stage, DEF-R1). A record's own `decision`/`overall_status` disposition never lives in this array -- only the join to the record that carries it; this array is coverage/join plumbing, never a clearance value (D7).
